pattern: (?i)\b(hand (this |it )?off|hand-?off|pick (this |it )?up (later|tomorrow)|pick up where (i|we) left off|pass (this |it |the rest )?to (another|a fresh|a new|the next) (agent|session|window|model)|continue (this )?(later|tomorrow|in a (new|fresh) session)|resume (this )?(later|tomorrow)|running (low|out) (on|of) context|wrap(ping)? up for (today|now|the day)|closing (this |the )?session)\b
message: handoff/resume intent detected. If work is IN FLIGHT: drop a baton — a verified handoff file in .brainer/baton/ (skills/baton/SKILL.md; Iron Rule: build State of Play from `git status`, never chat narrative). If PICKING UP: grab the newest with `ls -t .brainer/baton/`. Advisory — skip if the task is finished.

pattern: (?i)(?=.*\b(?:file|folder|dir|directory|path|export|output|render|asset|minis?|miniatures?|version|docs?|document|image|deliverable)\b)(?=(?:(?!\b(?:regex|assertion|function|stack trace|variable|import|class|unit test)\b).)*$|.*\b(?:I generated|we made|I created|earlier|yesterday|before the|last session)\b)\b(?:where (?:is|are|'?s)|which file (?:has|had|contains)|which (?:folder|dir|directory) (?:holds|contains)|what was the|can(?:'|no)t find|cannot find|couldn'?t find|where did (?:we|i|you) (?:put|leave|save|store))\b
message: this prompt is a where-is/what-was/can't-find memory-recall question about a project artifact. If a baton was grabbed this session (or .brainer/baton/ holds a handoff file — check with `ls -t .brainer/baton/`), RE-CONSULT the active baton's State of Play before answering from your own memory/assumption — live incident: 'where are the washington miniatures?!' got answered from stale recollection instead of the baton that already had the verified location. A grabbed baton is the authoritative source, not a one-time read. Also fires on 'which folder/dir holds/contains', 'what was the ...', 'can't/cannot/couldn't find', and 'where did we/i/you put/leave/save/store' phrasings (e.g. 'where did we put the minis?').
_note: no DETECTORS kind can check filesystem state (whether .brainer/baton/ actually holds a file) from inside the transcript-only detector contract — this probe mechanically catches the where-is/what-was/can't-find PROMPT PATTERN (prompt_intent, same mechanism as user_correction, which has no unless_pattern support — the require/exclude split is therefore baked into `pattern` itself via lookaheads) and folds the .brainer/baton/ precondition into the message text itself, so the agent (which CAN check the filesystem) decides applicability. Honest degradation, matching requirements-ledger's ledger-not-materialized precedent. DESIGN (2026-07-07 rev, replacing the 60-char negative-lookahead-window design): a POSITIVE artifact-noun anchor (file/folder/dir/path/export/output/render/asset/mini(s|ature)?/version/doc/image/deliverable — must appear ANYWHERE in the prompt) is now REQUIRED to fire at all, closing the old over-fire on artifact-less recall questions (e.g. 'where is the meeting?' no longer fires — no artifact noun). The code-debug-noun exclusion (regex/assertion/function/stack trace/variable/import/class/unit test) applies ONLY when NO past-work marker (I generated, we made, I created, earlier, yesterday, before the, last session) is present anywhere in the prompt — so 'which file contains the regex?' stays silent (debug noun, no past marker) but 'where is the export I generated before the regex refactor?' still fires (debug noun present, but so is a past-work marker, so the exclusion is lifted). A 'where did we/i/you put/leave/save/store' recall shape (e.g. 'where did we put the minis?') is now a recognized recall shape alongside where-is/which-file/what-was/can't-find. RESIDUAL FALSE-POSITIVE SURFACE (documented, not fixed): a genuine past-work recall question naming BOTH an artifact noun and a debug noun with NO past-work marker phrase in the prompt itself (e.g. relying on prior turns for context) will still stay silent even if it is in fact about baton-tracked work — the detector cannot see prior-turn context, only the current prompt string. ADVISORY heuristic over unbounded natural language — observed-incident phrasings + common variants are covered and regression-tested (3 adversarial rounds); paraphrase false-negatives and topic-collision false-positives remain possible by design and are accepted (LEARNING_CONTRACT §3: heuristic banked with scope of validity).
